subject: DevOps
title: Image Scanning
description: A short lesson to help you apply Image Scanning.

hook.text: You pulled a popular image from Docker Hub. It had three critical CVEs. You deployed it without knowing.

buildup.text: Images contain OS packages and libraries. Each one might have known vulnerabilities tracked in public databases.

discovery.text: Image scanners check every package against CVE databases. They report severity: low, medium, high, critical.

twist.text: Scanning should happen in the CI pipeline, not after deployment. Block vulnerable images from ever reaching production.

climax.text: Trivy, Grype, and Snyk Container scan images in seconds. Integrate them as pipeline quality gates.

punchline.text: Scan every image before it deploys. Known vulnerabilities are preventable.

quiz.question: When should container image scanning happen in the workflow?
options: In the CI pipeline before the image reaches production
Only after the image has been running for a week
Once a year during security audits
